name: Falscher Hase (False Hare (German Meatloaf))
recipeInstructions:
1. Thoroughly mix ground meats, onion, bread crumbs, 3 tablespoons cold water, and eggs.
2. Flavor with salt, paprika, mustard, and parsley.
3. Blend ingredients thoroughly.
4. Flatten out the meat mixture in a square shape (8 X 8-inches).
5. Arrange whole hard-boiled eggs in a row along the middle of the meat.
6. Fold sides of the meat patty over the eggs.
7. Shape the meat carefully into a loaf resembling a flat bread loaf.
8. Rinse hands occasionally in cold water to prevent sticking.
9. Cube 2 strips of bacon; cook in a Dutch oven for about 2 minutes.
10. Carefully add vegetable oil and heat.
11. Place the meatloaf in the Dutch oven and cook until browned on all sides.
12. Cut remaining bacon strips into halves and arrange over the top of the meatloaf.
13. Place the Dutch oven in a preheated oven for about 45 minutes.
14. While the meat is baking, gradually pour hot beef broth over the meatloaf and brush occasionally with pan drippings.
15. Remove the meat to a preheated platter and keep it warm.
16. Add 1/4 cup of hot water to the pan and scrape all particles from the bottom.
17. Bring to a gentle boil and add cornstarch mixed with 1/4 cup water.
18. Cook until bubbly and thick.
19. Remove from heat and stir in sour cream.
20. Reheat to warm.
21. Season with salt and pepper if desired.
22. Serve the sauce separately.
